2606.19371.title: ProMUSE: Progressive Multi-modal Uncertainty-guided Staged Evidential Alzheimer Disease Classification
2606.19371.paper_summary: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a fatal disorder that destroys memory and cognitive skills in the elderly population. Most treatments for AD are effective in the early stage, leading to an increasing demand for early AD diagnosis. AD diagnosis increasingly relies on multimodal data such as clinical assessments, structural Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), and Positron Emission Tomography (PET) imaging. However, MRI and PET acquisition remain costly and not universally accessible, making full-modality inference impractical in real-world clinical workflows. We propose ProMUSE, a Progressive Multi-modal Uncertainty Guided Staged Evidential Network that adaptively determines when additional modalities are necessary, helping reduce the overall cost of data acquisition while maintaining accuracy. ProMUSE first performs evidential classification using low-cost clinical data and quantifies uncertainty via a Dirichlet-based subjective logic model. When uncertainty exceeds a learned threshold, ProMUSE progressively incorporates MRI or PET features, fusing modality-wise belief and uncertainty through Dempster-Shafer theory to obtain a calibrated multimodal prediction. This staged acquisition strategy enables accurate diagnosis while minimizing reliance on expensive imaging. Experiments on ADNI, AIBL, and OASIS across CN-AD, CN-MCI, and MCI-AD tasks demonstrate that ProMUSE achieves competitive or superior accuracy compared to full-modality baselines while reducing MRI/PET usage by 50-90%, yielding substantial cost savings. These results highlight ProMUSE as a practical, uncertainty-aware, and resource-efficient solution for real-world AD screening.
2606.19371.paper_summary_zh: 阿茲海默症（AD）是一種致命的疾病，會摧毀老年人口的記憶和認知技能。大多數針對AD的治療在早期階段有效，因此對早期AD診斷的需求日益增加。AD的診斷越來越依賴於多模態數據，如臨床評估、結構性磁共振成像（MRI）和正電子發射斷層掃描（PET）成像。然而，MRI和PET的獲取仍然成本高昂且不普遍可及，使得在現實臨床工作流程中進行全模態推斷變得不切實際。我們提出了ProMUSE，一種漸進式多模態不確定性引導的分階段證據網絡，能夠自適應地確定何時需要額外的模態，幫助降低數據獲取的整體成本，同時保持準確性。ProMUSE首先使用低成本的臨床數據進行證據分類，並通過基於Dirichlet的主觀邏輯模型量化不確定性。當不確定性超過學習的閾值時，ProMUSE逐步納入MRI或PET特徵，通過Dempster-Shafer理論融合模態信念和不確定性，以獲得校準的多模態預測。這種分階段的獲取策略能夠實現準確診斷，同時最小化對昂貴成像的依賴。在CN-AD、CN-MCI和MCI-AD任務上對ADNI、AIBL和OASIS的實驗表明，ProMUSE在準確性上達到了與全模態基準相當或更優的水平，同時將MRI/PET的使用減少了50-90%，帶來了可觀的成本節省。這些結果突顯了ProMUSE作為一種實用的、關注不確定性且資源高效的現實AD篩查解決方案。
2606.19371.author: Long Doan et.al.
2606.19371.authors: Long Doan, Branden Chen, Ethan Litton, Huan Huang, Jiajing Huang, Yixin Xie, Weihua Zhou, Nandakumar Narayanan, Chen Zhao
